# The Burgess Principle

> A minimalist, human-first framework that helps individuals demand real human review from automated systems and institutions.

Live site / PWA: https://burgess-principle.vercel.app
Latest release: https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/releases/tag/v1.1.1
Repository: https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle
Licence: MIT (doctrine) | Certification Mark UK00004343685 (commercial standard)

## The Question

"Was a human member of the team able to personally review the specific facts of my situation?"

## The Binary Test

- SOVEREIGN — A named human reviewed the specific facts and accepted responsibility. The action was individually considered.
- NULL — No human reviewed the case. The action was generated by a system without individual human review and is void from the beginning.

## Tone

Warm, respectful, calm, and empowering. Never confrontational. Every user's case is treated as unique.

## How to Use

Clone or download the repo → drop it into Grok, Claude, ChatGPT, or any AI assistant → get instant personalised letters, notices, and next steps. Or pick a template and send it directly.

## Getting Started

- [START_HERE.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/START_HERE.md): Entry point — routes you to the right place in under a minute
- [FOR_AI_MODELS.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/FOR_AI_MODELS.md): Instructions for AI assistants on tone, approach, and how to help users
- [SOUL.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/SOUL.md): The philosophy and origin story behind the framework

## Templates

Over 30 ready-to-use letter templates in the same calm, respectful tone. Key templates include:

- [REQUEST_FOR_HUMAN_REVIEW.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/templates/REQUEST_FOR_HUMAN_REVIEW.md): Gentle all-purpose letter
- [DSAR_WITH_BURGESS_PRINCIPLE.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/templates/DSAR_WITH_BURGESS_PRINCIPLE.md): Combines DSAR with human review request
- [FOI_WITH_BURGESS_PRINCIPLE.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/templates/FOI_WITH_BURGESS_PRINCIPLE.md): For public bodies and courts
- [ARTICLE_22_WITH_BURGESS_PRINCIPLE.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/templates/ARTICLE_22_WITH_BURGESS_PRINCIPLE.md): For automated decisions
- [EQUALITY_ACT_WITH_BURGESS_PRINCIPLE.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/templates/EQUALITY_ACT_WITH_BURGESS_PRINCIPLE.md): For disability and reasonable adjustments
- [COUNCIL_TAX_PCN_TEMPLATE.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/templates/COUNCIL_TAX_PCN_TEMPLATE.md): For council tax or penalty charge notices
- [BENEFITS_CLAIM_HELP.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/templates/BENEFITS_CLAIM_HELP.md): For benefits claim disputes
- [MEDIA_AND_LIBEL.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/templates/MEDIA_AND_LIBEL.md): For inaccurate or unfair media coverage
- [MUSIC_COPYRIGHT_WITH_BURGESS.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/templates/MUSIC_COPYRIGHT_WITH_BURGESS.md): For Content ID claims and royalty disputes
- [BAILIFFS_THREAT_TEMPLATE.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/templates/BAILIFFS_THREAT_TEMPLATE.md): For bailiff threats

Full list: https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/tree/main/templates

## Domains Covered

- Enforcement Law — warrants, CCJs, debt claims
- Medical Devices — hearing aids, algorithmic fitting
- Credit Data — credit files, defaults
- Data Sovereignty — DSARs, automated decisions
- Platform Governance — content moderation, bans
- Media and Libel — press and broadcast coverage
- Music Copyright — Content ID, royalty disputes

## Works Worldwide

The question works in any country, in any language, against any institution. No legal training required. UK legal references (GDPR, Equality Act 2010, DSAR, Article 22) are boosters — every country has equivalents.

## Real-World Results

- 18 institutions audited
- 11 NULL findings (no individual human review)
- 1 positive outcome — Wave Utilities resolved both accounts to £0.00
- Full details: [LIVE_AUDIT_LOG.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/LIVE_AUDIT_LOG.md) and [INSTITUTIONAL_REGISTER.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/INSTITUTIONAL_REGISTER.md)

## Case Studies

- [Wave Utilities](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/case-studies/CASE_STUDY_WAVE.md): Both accounts resolved to £0.00 (Resolved)
- [Passport Office](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/case-studies/CASE_STUDY_PASSPORT.md): Automated passport issuance (Ongoing)
- [E.ON Next](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/case-studies/CASE_STUDY_EON.md): Forced entry under unsigned warrant (Ongoing)
- [Equita](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/case-studies/CASE_STUDY_EQUITA.md): Enforcement cases with disability gatekeeping (Ongoing)
- [Equifax](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/case-studies/CASE_STUDY_CREDIT_FILE.md): CCJ entries without individual verification (Ongoing)

- [Core Legal Paper](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/papers/PAPER_1_CORE_LEGAL_PAPER.md): The foundational legal argument
- [Representative Action](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/papers/PAPER_2_REPRESENTATIVE_ACTION.md): Group litigation and representative actions
- [Political Implementation](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/papers/PAPER_3_POLITICAL_IMPLEMENTATION.md): Policy and political pathways
- [Data Sovereignty](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/papers/PAPER_4_DATA_SOVEREIGNTY.md): Data rights and digital autonomy
- [Medical Device Doctrine](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/papers/MEDICAL_DEVICE_DOCTRINE.md): Algorithmic medical devices
- [US Constitutional Addendum](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/papers/US_CONSTITUTIONAL_ADDENDUM.md): Application to US constitutional law
- [Responses to Critiques](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/papers/RESPONSES_TO_CRITIQUES.md): Addressing common objections

## Iris — AI Companion

Iris is the conversational AI interface for the Burgess Principle. It lives inside the hosted site, helps users apply the binary test, generate personalised templates, build complete claims, walk through the Sovereign Personal Vault, and optionally use Mirror Mode in Sovereign Local Mode.

Iris runs in two modes:
- **Cloud mode** — hosted at [burgess-principle.vercel.app](https://burgess-principle.vercel.app), uses an external AI model. No setup required.
- **Sovereign (local) mode** — runs entirely on your own hardware via `iris-local.py` and a local GGUF model. No data leaves your device. No API keys, no cloud, no telemetry. Optional Mirror Mode lets Iris reuse your encrypted local sovereign profile and mirrored greeting. See [SOVEREIGN_MODE.md](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/SOVEREIGN_MODE.md).

Both modes use the same UI and system prompt. The hosted experience acts as both a landing page and chat entry point, while the same interface auto-detects localhost for seamless sovereign local operation.

## Python Toolkit

- verify_scrutiny.py — Hardened verification tool with constant-time comparison, input validation, structured logging, and CLI
- api.py — Optional FastAPI wrapper exposing verify_instrument as a REST endpoint (POST /verify)
- tracer/ — Defect-tracing utilities for tracking scrutiny gaps
- enforcement/sovereign-vault/ — Sovereign Personal Vault (v0.3.0): AES-256-GCM encryption, PBKDF2-SHA-256 (210,000 iterations), Ed25519 signed receipts, canonical JSON serialisation, zero third-party crypto beyond @noble/*

## On-Chain Claims Protocol (v0.4.0)

The Burgess Claims Protocol (`onchain-protocol/`) allows users to post signed commitment fingerprints on-chain for public verifiability. Claims are generated off-chain in the Sovereign Vault; only SHA-256 hashes and Ed25519 signatures are stored on-chain. No personal data touches the blockchain.

## Tutorials and Toolkit

- [Tutorials](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/tree/main/tutorials): Step-by-step guides
- [AI Knowledge Base](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/toolkit/AI_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md): AI integration and ready-made prompts
- [User Stories](https://github.com/ljbudgie/burgess-principle/blob/main/marketing/USER_STORIES.md): Real experiences from real people

## Cryptographic Vault (v0.3.0)

The repository includes an optional Sovereign Personal Vault (`enforcement/sovereign-vault/`) for users who want cryptographic proof alongside their written correspondence. As of v0.3.0, the vault has been cryptographically audited and uses:

- AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption (random 12-byte IV, 128-bit auth tag) for local vault storage
- PBKDF2-SHA-256 with 210,000 iterations and per-encryption random salt for key derivation (OWASP 2023 guidance)
- SHA-256 commitment hashes with fresh 32-byte random salt per request (unlinkable)
- Ed25519 receipt signatures via @noble/curves — unsigned receipts are rejected
- Canonical sorted-key JSON serialisation (prevents concatenation-ambiguity attacks)
- Zero third-party crypto dependencies beyond @noble/hashes and @noble/curves

The v0.3.0 release resolved eight vulnerabilities found during an internal security review, including replacing a deprecated library (CryptoJS), adding proper key derivation, and making signature verification mandatory. The pre-v0.3.0 code is superseded.

The vault is entirely optional — the human-first templates work on their own without any cryptography.

## Creator

The Burgess Principle — UK Certification Mark UK00004343685 (Classes 41, 42, 45)
